title: Migration / DB ownership decision gate

current_runtime_truth.go_current_role: Read Schema_Meta for health/schema version verification only

ownership_options.scope: Python remains the migration runner for app startup, Pi deployment, production DB upgrades, and the public migration status endpoint.
ownership_options.reason: Current migration chain includes Python-specific detection, idempotent duplicate-column/no-such-column handling, and rollback behavior already covered by existing pytest; keeping it avoids duplicating high-risk DB mutation semantics in Go before full proof.

ownership_options.scope: Go may expose local/read-only migration readiness/status for copied DB or packaged local smoke, reading Schema_Meta and compiled expected version without applying SQL.
required_guards: No DDL or DML migration SQL execution
Response parity with Python current/latest/pending semantics
Copied DB only until a separate live route gate

ownership_options.scope: Do not implement or route Go migration execution in 23.7.
blocked_until: Go can reproduce the full ordered migration list and idempotent skip semantics
Fresh DB and upgraded DB fixtures prove identical final schema
Failed migration rollback leaves Schema_Meta and affected tables consistent
Production backup and restore drill is documented
Pi preflight and rollback commands are tested
Python fallback owner is explicitly retained until live proof passes

schema_safety_contract.idempotency: Any future runner must safely re-run completed migrations and skip duplicate columns / already-dropped columns without changing final schema.
schema_safety_contract.version_table: Schema_Meta key=schema_version remains the single schema version source of truth.
schema_safety_contract.pending_detection: pending = migrations with version > current_version; latest_version = highest declared migration version.
failed_migration_recovery: Failed migration must rollback the current transaction
Schema_Meta must not advance past the failed version
Partial DDL/DML effects must be detected in a copied DB fixture before any live route
Python migration runner remains the recovery owner until a dedicated Go runner gate closes

not_authorized_by_23_7: Go DDL or migration DML execution

allowed_next_step.title: Local packaging execution track
allowed_next_step.scope: Define local packaged artifact boundaries for Go binary, bundled frontend dist, external data dir, config/env/logs/uploads paths, SQLite WAL behavior, and migration ownership. Do not run production migration or deploy Pi.
